title: Doric Weekly Market Insight
summary:
"The sixteenth week ended with mixed feelings, as iron ore futures and Baltic indices moved towards diametrically opposite directions."
By Michalis Voutsinas
Following World Trade Organization's rather bearish view of 1.7 percent merchandise trade volume growth and IMF's downgraded projection of global economic growth, China's faster than expected expansion during Q1 injected moderate optimism in the dry bulk market. Leaving the various holidays behind, the second part of the sixteenth trading week was quite positive for the Baltic indices, with Supramaxes being in the front seat. In fact, the capricious Capes moved $926 higher, ending today at $16,270 daily.
